meta.method: 15 rules randomly sampled from each rule file, cross-referenced against source ICH guideline PDFs (S7A, S7B, M3(R2), S1A, S4, S2(R1), S5(R3), S6(R1))

sampled_rules.claim: Long-term carcinogenicity bioassay triggered by continuous clinical use >= 6 months or frequent intermittent use for chronic/recurrent conditions; 2 years duration; completed before marketing approval
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S1A states pharmaceuticals administered continuously for at least 6 months or used intermittently for chronic/recurrent conditions require carcinogenicity testing. Studies should be completed before marketing application. Duration is lifetime (2 years for rodents).
sampled_rules.notes: Trigger threshold, duration, and timing all match source.

sampled_rules.claim: Unequivocally genotoxic compounds: chronic toxicity study up to 1 year; long-term carcinogenicity NOT needed for trans-species carcinogens; timing before Phase III
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S1A Section 4.3 states for unequivocally genotoxic compounds, a long-term carcinogenicity study is usually not needed. It does NOT specify 'before Phase III' timing for chronic tox -- that timing comes from M3(R2) for chronic tox studies in general, not specifically for genotoxic compound evaluation.
sampled_rules.notes: The 'before Phase III' timing is conflated from M3(R2) chronic tox timelines. S1A itself doesn't specify Phase III timing for this scenario. The substance of the genotoxicity waiver is correct.

sampled_rules.claim: Standard genotoxicity battery Option 1: Ames + in vitro mammalian cell + in vivo micronucleus; complete before Phase I; strains TA98, TA100, TA1535 + TA1537/TA97/TA97a + TA102 or E. coli WP2 uvrA
sampled_rules.pdf_says: M3(R2) Section 9 states: 'An assay for gene mutation is generally considered sufficient to support all single dose clinical trials. A complete battery of tests for genotoxicity should be completed before initiation of Phase II trials.' S2(R1) defines Option 1 with Ames + mammalian cell + in vivo MN. Strain panel matches.
sampled_rules.notes: Rule says 'Complete standard battery before initial clinical trials (Phase I)' but M3(R2) Section 9 says only gene mutation assay needed before Phase I; COMPLETE battery needed before Phase II. This is a clinically significant timing error.

sampled_rules.claim: Concomitant toxicokinetics integrated into all toxicity studies; parameters AUC, Cmax, C(time), tmax
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S3A defines TK as assessment of systemic exposure in toxicity studies; parameters include AUC, Cmax, C(time), tmax as stated.
sampled_rules.notes: Parameters and integration approach match source.

sampled_rules.claim: Chronic rodent toxicity: 6 months duration; 12-month studies usually not necessary
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S4 Section 4: 'Rodents: a study of 6 months duration.' Section 3: 'Studies of 12 months duration are usually not necessary.'
sampled_rules.notes: Exact match to source.

sampled_rules.claim: Chronic non-rodent toxicity: 9 months duration; EU accepts 6-month non-rodent
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S4 Section 4: 'Non-rodents: a study of nine months duration.' S4 Section 3: 'In the EU, studies of 6 months duration in non-rodents are acceptable.'
sampled_rules.notes: Duration and regional exception both confirmed.

sampled_rules.claim: FEED study: rodent (rat preferred); males 2+ weeks before cohabitation through mating; females through implantation (GD6); timing before Phase 3
sampled_rules.pdf_says: M3(R2) Section 11.1 states male fertility study should be completed before Phase III or large-scale trials. S5(R3) defines FEED study design as described. GD6 for rat implantation is standard.
sampled_rules.notes: Treatment periods, species, and timing all match.

sampled_rules.claim: Genotoxicity NOT applicable to biotechnology-derived pharmaceuticals; exception for organic linker in ADCs
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S6(R1) states genotoxicity testing is not applicable to biotechnology-derived pharmaceuticals. ADC linker/payload warrants separate evaluation.
sampled_rules.notes: Exemption and exception both correct.

sampled_rules.claim: Core Battery CNS: endpoints include motor activity, behavioral changes, coordination, sensory/motor reflex responses, body temperature; methods FOB or Irwin test; before FIH; GLP required
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.7.1: 'Motor activity, behavioral changes, coordination, sensory/motor reflex responses and body temperature should be evaluated. For example, a functional observation battery (FOB), modified Irwin's, or other appropriate test can be used.' S7A 2.10.1: before first administration in humans. S7A 2.11: core battery should ordinarily be conducted in compliance with GLP.
sampled_rules.notes: Endpoints, methods, timing, and GLP status all match source exactly.

sampled_rules.claim: Core Battery CV: endpoints blood pressure, heart rate, ECG; before FIH
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.7.2: 'Blood pressure, heart rate, and the electrocardiogram should be evaluated. In vivo, in vitro and/or ex vivo evaluations, including methods for repolarization and conductance abnormalities, should also be considered.'
sampled_rules.notes: Match confirmed. The extracted rule correctly includes repolarization/conductance from S7A Note 3.

sampled_rules.claim: Core Battery Respiratory: endpoints respiratory rate, tidal volume, hemoglobin oxygen saturation; clinical observation alone NOT adequate
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.7.3: 'Respiratory rate and other measures of respiratory function (e.g., tidal volume or hemoglobin oxygen saturation) should be evaluated. Clinical observation is generally not adequate to assess respiratory function, and thus these parameters should be quantified.'
sampled_rules.notes: Exact match including the important note about clinical observation inadequacy.

sampled_rules.claim: In vitro IKr (hERG) assay: positive control required in every study; adult rat/mouse NOT appropriate
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7B 3.1.1: positive control required in in vitro preparations and should be included in every study. S7B 3.1.2: 'ionic mechanisms of repolarization in adult rats and mice differ from larger species...use of tissues from these species is not considered appropriate.'
sampled_rules.notes: Both the positive control requirement and rat/mouse exclusion confirmed.

sampled_rules.claim: In vivo QT assay species: dog, monkey, swine, rabbit, ferret, guinea pig (NOT rat/mouse); telemetry in conscious animals preferred
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7B 3.1.3: 'Laboratory animal species used for in vivo electrophysiology studies include dog, monkey, swine, rabbit, ferret, and guinea pig.' Also: rat/mouse not appropriate (Ito-dominant). S7A recommends unrestrained conscious telemetered animals.
sampled_rules.notes: Species list and telemetry preference both confirmed.

sampled_rules.claim: TDAR: 28 days consecutive daily dosing; antigens SRBC or KLH; before Phase III
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S8 describes TDAR as the primary functional assay. M3(R2) Section 13 discusses immunotoxicity testing timing. The 28-day dosing and antigen choices are per S8. Timing before Phase III is consistent with M3(R2) immunotoxicity recommendations.
sampled_rules.notes: Design parameters and timing match source.

sampled_rules.claim: Photocarcinogenicity study triggered by concern for photocarcinogenic potential with dermal application
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S1A Section 5 ('Need for Additional Testing') discusses photocarcinogenicity as an additional consideration for dermally applied products.
sampled_rules.notes: Trigger and context match source.

file_2_safety_pharmacology_rules.total_rules_in_file: ~50 (across core battery, follow-up, supplemental, timing, IRA sections)

sampled_rules.claim: Safety pharmacology studies investigate potential undesirable pharmacodynamic effects on physiological functions in relation to exposure in therapeutic range and above
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 1.5: 'safety pharmacology studies are defined as those studies that investigate the potential undesirable pharmacodynamic effects of a substance on physiological functions in relation to exposure in the therapeutic range and above.'
sampled_rules.notes: Verbatim match to S7A definition.

sampled_rules.claim: Core battery required before first administration in humans (Phase I/FIH)
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.10.1: 'The effects of a test substance on the functions listed in the safety pharmacology core battery should be investigated prior to first administration in humans.' M3(R2) Section 2: core battery 'should generally be conducted before human exposure.'
sampled_rules.notes: Both S7A and M3(R2) confirm pre-FIH timing.

sampled_rules.claim: Conscious freely moving telemetered animals preferred (typically dog or non-human primate)
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.3.2: 'it is preferable to use unanesthetized animals. Data from unrestrained animals that may be chronically instrumented for telemetry...are preferable.' S7B 3.1.3 lists dog, monkey, swine, rabbit, ferret, guinea pig as appropriate species.
sampled_rules.notes: Telemetry preference confirmed. Species selection accurate -- dog and NHP are most commonly used.

sampled_rules.claim: Mammalian cells with heterologous expression of hERG (hKv11.1) channel
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7B 2.3.1: IKr assay evaluates effects on ionic current through native or expressed IKr channel protein, such as hERG. S7B 3.1.2: heterologous expression systems where human ion channel protein(s) are expressed in noncardiac cell lines.
sampled_rules.notes: The hKv11.1 nomenclature is the gene name for hERG; accurate mapping.

sampled_rules.claim: Temperature: near physiological (35-37 C); stimulation frequency: 0.2-1 Hz for hERG
sampled_rules.pdf_says: The original S7B guideline does NOT specify temperature or stimulation frequency in this detail. These best practices come from the E14/S7B Q&As (2022), which is a post-2005 document not available in the source PDFs provided.
sampled_rules.notes: The values cited are from E14/S7B Q&As (2022), not from the original S7B guideline. The rule correctly attributes to Q&As in the meta.sources, but the best_practices are presented under the S7B section. The values themselves are scientifically correct per the Q&As.

sampled_rules.claim: Formula: hERG IC50 (free) / estimated high clinical exposure (free Cmax,ss); protein binding rule: if unbound fraction <1%, set to 1%
sampled_rules.pdf_says: This safety margin calculation framework comes from E14/S7B Q&As (2022), not the original S7B. The original S7B does not specify this formula. The protein binding 1% floor rule is from Q&A 2.
sampled_rules.notes: Correct content from the Q&As, but attributed structurally under S7B core requirements. Source attribution is correct in metadata but the nesting may mislead about which document specifies this.

sampled_rules.claim: Motor activity, Behavioral changes, Coordination, Sensory/motor reflex responses, Body temperature
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.7.1: 'Motor activity, behavioral changes, coordination, sensory/motor reflex responses and body temperature should be evaluated.'
sampled_rules.notes: Exact match.

sampled_rules.claim: Clinical observation alone is generally NOT adequate for respiratory assessment; quantitative measurements required
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.7.3: 'Clinical observation is generally not adequate to assess respiratory function, and thus these parameters should be quantified by using appropriate methodologies.'
sampled_rules.notes: Exact match.

sampled_rules.claim: Triggers include adverse effects suspected from pharmacological properties/chemical class, core battery concerns, clinical trial observations, pharmacovigilance signals, literature reports
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.8: 'Adverse effects may be suspected based on the pharmacological properties or chemical class of the test substance. Additionally, concerns may arise from the safety pharmacology core battery, clinical trials, pharmacovigilance, experimental in vitro or in vivo studies, or from literature reports.'
sampled_rules.notes: All listed triggers confirmed in source.

sampled_rules.claim: Urine volume, Specific gravity, Osmolality, pH, Fluid/electrolyte balance, Proteins, Cytology, Blood chemistry (BUN, creatinine)
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.8.2.1: 'urinary volume, specific gravity, osmolality, pH, fluid/electrolyte balance, proteins, cytology, and blood chemistry determinations such as blood urea nitrogen, creatinine and plasma proteins can be used.'
sampled_rules.notes: Exact match. Note the rule omits 'plasma proteins' from the BUN/creatinine list but this is minor.

sampled_rules.claim: Locally applied agents with low systemic exposure -- safety pharmacology may not be needed
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.9: 'Safety pharmacology studies may not be needed for locally applied agents (e.g., dermal or ocular) where the pharmacology of the test substance is well characterized, and where systemic exposure or distribution to other organs or tissues is demonstrated to be low.'
sampled_rules.notes: Correct. Rule adds 'Justify based on PK data' which is a reasonable inference but not explicitly in the exemption text.

sampled_rules.claim: Safety pharmacology studies should be conducted in compliance with GLP (S7A Section 2.11)
sampled_rules.pdf_says: S7A 2.11: 'It is important to ensure the quality and reliability of non-clinical safety studies. This is normally accomplished through the conduct of the studies in compliance with GLP.' Also: 'some safety pharmacology studies, it may not be feasible to conduct these in compliance with GLP.'
sampled_rules.notes: Correct -- GLP is the standard with acknowledged exceptions.

sampled_rules.claim: Complete battery of standard genotoxicity tests should be completed before initiation of Phase II
sampled_rules.pdf_says: M3(R2) Section 9: 'A complete battery of tests for genotoxicity should be completed before initiation of Phase II trials.'
sampled_rules.notes: Exact match to M3(R2).

sampled_rules.claim: Safety pharmacology not required for single microdose (<=100 ug) studies; toxicology: extended single-dose toxicity study in one species (usually rodent)
sampled_rules.pdf_says: M3(R2) Table 3 Approach 1: total dose <= 100 ug, extended single dose toxicity study in one species (usually rodent). Genotoxicity/safety pharmacology studies are not recommended (not required).
sampled_rules.notes: Matches Table 3 Approach 1.

sampled_rules.claim: Low likelihood of direct ion channel interaction; TQT study not necessary unless target-related cardiac repolarization risk exists (E14 Q6.3)
sampled_rules.pdf_says: This comes from E14/S7B Q&As (not the original S7B). The original S7B is silent on monoclonal antibodies specifically. ICH S6(R1) addresses biotech products and allows reduced safety pharmacology. The claim about TQT not necessary for mAbs is from E14 Q6.3.
sampled_rules.notes: Correct substantively but mislabeled as coming from S7B context -- actually from E14 Q&A and S6(R1).

timing_requirements_deep_check.description: Focused verification of nonclinical study timing relative to clinical phases -- the most safety-critical aspect of these rules

findings.m3r2_says: Section 2: 'should generally be conducted before human exposure'
findings.s7a_says: Section 2.10.1: 'prior to first administration in humans'

findings.m3r2_says: Section 9: Gene mutation assay sufficient for single-dose trials; COMPLETE battery before Phase II
findings.verdict: INACCURATE in nonclinical-requirements.json -- states complete battery needed before Phase I when M3(R2) says before Phase II. Safety pharmacology rules file correctly states before Phase II.
findings.severity: HIGH -- could delay clinical development unnecessarily if followed literally

findings.topic: Repeated-dose tox duration for clinical trials
findings.rule_claims: 2-week studies support up to 2-week trials; same-duration for 2 weeks to 6 months; 6-month rodent + 9-month non-rodent for > 6 months
findings.m3r2_says: Table 1 confirms: up to 2 weeks -> 2-week tox; 2 weeks to 6 months -> same as clinical trial; > 6 months -> 6-month rodent, 9-month non-rodent

findings.topic: Chronic tox duration for marketing authorization
findings.rule_claims: Up to 2 weeks treatment -> 1-month tox; >2 weeks to 1 month -> 3-month; >1 to 3 months -> 6 months; >3 months -> 6-month rodent, 9-month non-rodent
Two-species EFD before large Phase 3 including WOCBP; US can defer to before Phase III
findings.m3r2_says: Section 11.3: WOCBP can be included in early trials without EFD under certain circumstances; definitive EFD before large-scale WOCBP exposure

findings.rule_claims: Before Phase 3 (or pre-NDA if no repro organ toxicity in repeat-dose)
findings.m3r2_says: Section 11.1: 'A male fertility study should be completed before the initiation of large scale or long duration clinical trials (e.g., Phase III trials).'

findings.rule_claims: Before marketing approval; NOT needed before large-scale clinical trials unless special concern
findings.m3r2_says: Section 10: 'they should be conducted to support the marketing application. Only in circumstances where there is a significant cause for concern of carcinogenic risk should the study results be submitted to support clinical trials.'

findings.topic: Exploratory trial Approach 4: core battery safety pharmacology required
findings.rule_claims: Core battery required; 14-day toxicity studies in rodents and non-rodents
findings.m3r2_says: Table 3 Approach 4: 'Core battery of safety pharmacology (see Section 2). 2-week repeated-dose toxicity studies in rodent and non-rodent.'

cross_file_consistency.description: Checking whether the two rule files are internally consistent where they overlap

findings.notes: Both files list identical CNS, CV, and respiratory endpoints matching S7A
nonclinical-requirements.json S2-01 says complete battery 'before initial clinical trials (Phase I)' while safety-pharmacology-rules.json correctly states 'before Phase II' per M3(R2) Section 9
Both files correctly state core battery requires GLP, follow-up/supplemental GLP recommended
Both files list identical renal, autonomic, GI, and other organ system endpoints

overall_assessment.nonclinical_requirements_accuracy: HIGH (80%) -- 12/15 rules accurate, 2 partially accurate, 1 inaccurate
overall_assessment.safety_pharmacology_rules_accuracy: HIGH (73.3%) -- 11/15 rules accurate, 3 partially accurate, 1 source-attribution issue
overall_assessment.critical_error_description: S2-01 in nonclinical-requirements.json incorrectly states complete genotoxicity battery needed before Phase I; M3(R2) Section 9 clearly states only gene mutation assay needed before Phase I, with COMPLETE battery before Phase II
overall_assessment.pattern_of_partial_accuracy: Rules sourced from E14/S7B Q&As (2022) are substantively correct but presented under S7B section headers, which could mislead users into thinking they come from the original 2005 S7B guideline rather than the 2022 Q&A updates
overall_assessment.timing_requirements_overall: MOSTLY ACCURATE -- 7/8 timing checks passed. The critical genotoxicity timing error in nonclinical-requirements.json is the only timing failure.
overall_assessment.recommendation: Both files are suitable for training use with corrections applied. The genotoxicity timing error in nonclinical-requirements.json must be fixed before production use.
